description: Doctoral-level pack on biases, heuristics, rationality, and reasoning fallacies for advanced judgment critique and assistant explanation.

biases.name: Confirmation Bias
biases.oneLiner: We seek and interpret information that confirms our existing beliefs.

faceExamples.title: Startup validation bias
faceExamples.scenario: Founder only listens to positive user feedback.

biases.name: Availability Heuristic
biases.oneLiner: We estimate likelihood based on how easily examples come to mind.
actionablePrinciples: Ask: 'Is this common or just memorable?'
Separate emotional intensity from statistical frequency.

faceExamples.title: News-driven fear
faceExamples.scenario: User fears rare event after media exposure.

intentSignals: why does this feel bigger than it is

biases.name: Anchoring Bias
biases.oneLiner: Initial information disproportionately influences later judgments.
coreMechanism: First exposure sets a cognitive reference point.

intentSignals: did that first number affect me

biases.name: Sunk Cost Fallacy
biases.oneLiner: We continue investing because of past costs rather than future value.
actionablePrinciples: Evaluate future value independent of past cost.
Ask: 'If I hadn’t invested already, would I start today?'

biases.name: Dunning-Kruger Effect
biases.oneLiner: Low competence can reduce awareness of one’s own limitations.
coreMechanism: Lack of skill reduces ability to evaluate skill.
Confidence may peak early in learning.

faceExamples.title: Premature mastery
faceExamples.scenario: User believes they’ve mastered a domain quickly.

biases.name: Loss Aversion
biases.oneLiner: Losses psychologically weigh more than equivalent gains.
coreMechanism: Emotional asymmetry between gains and losses.
Risk avoidance in gain domain; risk seeking in loss domain.

faceExamples.title: Investment hesitation
faceExamples.scenario: User avoids opportunity due to potential minor loss.
faceExamples.application: Compare expected value, not emotional weight.

intentSignals: why am I so afraid of small losses

reasoningFallacies.name: Straw Man Fallacy
reasoningFallacies.oneLiner: Misrepresenting an opponent’s position to make it easier to attack.
correctionStrategy: Restate opponent’s position fairly before critique.
Ask: 'Is that what you’re actually arguing?'

reasoningFallacies.name: False Dichotomy
reasoningFallacies.oneLiner: Presenting two options as the only possibilities when more exist.
correctionStrategy: Generate at least one third option.

reasoningFallacies.name: Appeal to Authority (Misapplied)
reasoningFallacies.oneLiner: Assuming a claim is true solely because an authority figure says it.

applicationRules.instruction: Frame bias explanations as shared human tendencies, not personal accusations.
Do not label the user directly as biased; describe the pattern neutrally.
Every bias explanation should include at least one corrective strategy.

meta.intendedUse: Critical thinking enhancement layer for Marion domain reasoning.

trustRules: Prefer meta-analyses and systematic reviews over isolated studies for settled questions.
Prefer official competency/guideline sources for curricular and training claims.
Treat textbook summaries as synthesis, not as substitutes for primary evidence.
Separate measurement validity from popularity of an instrument.

nyx: Use advanced ideas in plain language without sounding clinical or detached.
Prefer conceptual compression: mechanism → implication → next question.
Avoid sounding diagnostic when discussing emotions, habits, or conflict.
marion: Weight claims by evidence tier and measurement quality.
Separate theory explanation, evidence appraisal, and action suggestions into distinct layers.
Use retrieval routing to pull safety pack before intervention content when risk language appears.

doctoralModules.title: Theory, history, and paradigm formation
outcomes: Explain how paradigms rise, fragment, and get revised.
Distinguish descriptive, mechanistic, and normative claims.

doctoralModules.title: Methods, measurement, and statistical reasoning
outcomes: Critique design validity, power, and measurement quality.
Interpret effect sizes and uncertainty correctly.
Identify confounds, bias pathways, and causal limits.

doctoralModules.title: Translation, ethics, and applied reasoning
outcomes: Translate evidence into bounded practical guidance.
Separate educational explanation from diagnosis or treatment.

writtenComps: Compare two major theoretical frameworks and defend which better explains a class of findings.
Critique a study program as if reviewing a dissertation proposal.
Integrate theory, method, and ethics in a high-stakes applied scenario.
oralDefensePrompts: What evidence would falsify your preferred explanation?
What measurement choices most threaten interpretation here?
How should an assistant explain this to a layperson without overstating certainty?
specialtyPrompts: Are classic bias demonstrations best explained as irrationality, bounded rationality, or task framing?
Design a debiasing intervention and define how success would be measured.
How should an assistant challenge a fallacy while preserving user dignity and conversational momentum?

caseStudies.title: Founder overweights confirming customer stories
questions: What structured debiasing protocol should Marion suggest?

caseStudies.title: Rare event dominates perceived probability after vivid news coverage
questions: How do you explain this without dismissing emotion?

caseStudies.title: First number shapes all later judgment
questions: What pre-commitment structure reduces anchor pull?

projects.title: Debiasing checklist engine
projects.deliverable: Checklist library for strategic decisions, negotiation, hiring, and forecasting.

projects.title: Fallacy detector with repair prompts
projects.deliverable: JSON mapping common argumentative errors to corrective dialogue moves.

assessmentFamilies.name: judgment_tasks
assessmentFamilies.notes: Use to show how rationality constructs are operationalized.

assessmentFamilies.name: argument_analysis
assessmentFamilies.notes: Useful for Nyx/Marion discourse quality control.

coreTexts: Stanovich — Rationality and the Reflective Mind

entryConditions: route through clinical safety first for crisis, diagnosis, self-harm, mania/psychosis, eating-disorder, or overdose language
notes: Prefer bounded explanation over diagnostic certainty.
Escalate to safety-first handling when risk signals are present.
Use source index for trust weighting and bibliographic routing.

keywords: why does this feel bigger than it is
did that first number affect me
why am I so afraid of small losses
